page: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g-11520s is a virtual server instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 192 vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture and manufactured by AMD. The CPU resources are allocated on a shared basis. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

Local storage is bundled with the instance, consisting of 11,520 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. This high-capacity local storage provides substantial space for data-intensive operations. The server does not include any GPU hardware, making it unsuitable for workloads requiring hardware acceleration. It includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model allows for a high density of virtual cores and memory at a lower cost profile compared to dedicated hardware, though it introduces potential performance variability depending on host node utilization. This configuration is suitable for large-scale general-purpose workloads, massive databases, storage-heavy applications, and distributed data processing tasks that can tolerate shared CPU resources.
description: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g-11520s is a high-capacity virtual server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 192 shared AMD vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 11,520 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance does not feature GPU accelerators but includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. Because the CPU allocation is shared, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for resource-dense deployments. It is suited for large-scale general-purpose applications, high-capacity databases, and storage-heavy workloads that benefit from massive local NVMe capacity without requiring dedicated CPU cores.
